Text: I get the impression that he is a kind and supportive instructor. I think that the fact that he gives specific examples of areas for improvement makes this a very useful resource for medical students. I don't have any specific areas for improvement.
Code: Specific points for improvement

Text: I get the impression that he is trying to motivate students to “communicate” by separating the items into different categories. I feel that it would be more useful if he included more detailed examples of what students should learn. I feel that there are too many areas for improvement, and not enough praise for what students have learned and what they have written.
Code: Too many points for improvement/Lack of positive feedback

Text: I get the impression that he is trying to encourage students by first praising them for writing their records. As a positive point, there are specific descriptions of how to write entries that will help learning and enable goal setting. I don't think there are any major points that need to be improved.
Code: Specific points for improvement/Sufficient amount of positive feedback

Text: While praising the fact that they are writing records, there are many items for improvement in the records of medical students. A good point is that feedback is given for each item, so it is easy to understand. As for points that need to be improved, there were comments that what was learned was abstract, but I felt that there were also parts that were described in detail, and there seemed to be a gap between the medical students (those writing the records) and the evaluators.
Code: Too many points for improvement

Text: The impression was that there were many points listed for improvement. As a good point, the fact that the areas for improvement were described in concrete terms. As points for improvement, there were specific examples given, but as a medical student, I felt that these were too high a hurdle.
Code: Too many points for improvement/Specific points for improvement/Too high a demand/Need to set learning goals

Text: At a glance, I felt that there was a lot of feedback. As a good point, the fact that the feedback was given in a way that encouraged the student. As points for improvement, I felt that the feedback was generally too abstract.
Code: Too many points for improvement

Text: The impression is that detailed feedback is given on the student's record. The good points are that detailed examples are also included. The points that need improvement are that the feedback content seems to be a mixture of advanced parts and the level of medical students.
Code: Too high a demand/Need to set learning goals

Text: The feedback is long, but the impression is that it lacks concreteness. The good points are that it encourages learning about imaging tests. The points that need improvement are that it lacks concreteness.
Code: Lack of specificity

Text: The impression is that there are few details, but there are examples given. The good points are that it also includes detailed descriptions and gives specific examples. There are no points that need to be improved.
Code: Presentation of specific examples/Specific feedback

Text: I think that appropriate feedback is given. The good points are that it gives specific examples. I thought it would be good if there was feedback not only on future issues, but also on what was learned.
Code: Lack of recognition of what has been learned

Text: The content shows that the student has read and recorded the medical student's notes. The good points are that it suggests that learning will be enhanced if the procedure and precautions for the drain are described in more detail. The points for improvement are that, although there are detailed descriptions of what needs to be improved, there are no detailed descriptions of what the learner should be praised for.
Code: Lack of specificity in recognition

Text: The impression is that the points for improvement are described in relation to the content of the description. The good points are that it describes how to record patient information and what has been learned so that it can be linked to the next learning session. There are no points for improvement.
Code: Feedback on what has been written

Text: The impression is that advice is given on how medical students should record information. The good points are that detailed descriptions of entries that only include words such as “rounds” and “mark” are linked to learning. There are no particular areas for improvement.
Code: Feedback on what has been written

Text: The impression is that there are many areas for improvement. The good points are that the importance of anatomy and the fact that reviewing procedures when learning techniques is linked to learning are described. The areas for improvement are that, although there are instructions to look further into what has been learned, there is no feedback on whether the current entries are correct.
Code: Too many points for improvement

Text: The impression is that there are many points for improvement. The good points are that specific examples are given and the content is linked to the next stage of learning. The point for improvement is that there was no comment on nephrotic syndrome, which was listed as something that had been learned.
Code: Too many points for improvement/Lack of reference to what was learned

Text: The content shows that the learners are being supported. The good points are that the content listed is linked to the next stage of learning by adding specifics. The point for improvement is that there was no feedback on what had been learned.
Code: Lack of reference to what was learned

Text: There are many points for improvement. The good points are that it describes the importance of recording information in patient records. There are no points for improvement in this feedback form. However, it is inevitable that the content of the slides is not recorded in the records, but the fact that there is no feedback on the content of the slides is a point for improvement.
Code: Lack of reference to the content of the practical training

Text: (Good points) It starts with a positive message to the students. There are many specific suggestions. (Bad points) Although it is specific, it is a general message, and it cannot be said to be a message based on the students' specific experiences in the department.
Code: Deviation from the experience gained in the practical training

Text: (Bad point) It has been pointed out that the date is missing, but I don't think that is the case. If there is even one such incorrect entry, there is a possibility that the learner will not be able to properly receive the feedback. The suggestion to write “Start preparing for the presentation” as “Prepare for the presentation on a specific topic or patient” is also of little actual feedback value. (Good point) The example of asking the learner to write about what they specifically learned about dystrophy is good.
Code: Decrease in reliability due to incorrect points/Specific points

Text: It also suggests some specific areas for improvement along with supportive language. However, it gives the impression of being a comment made without seeing the actual situation, and I have some concerns about whether the content of the feedback really matches the experience gained by the student.
Code: Deviation from the experience gained in the practical training

Text: The comments are supportive and specific, and are based on a thorough review of the student's record. This student has filled out their record in sufficient detail, so the feedback is also more specific and personalized. On the other hand, it can be said of both AI feedback and human feedback in general, that AI feedback is limited to feedback on the student's actual written content. On the other hand, human feedback is more about what the student actually experienced in the field, rather than what they wrote down. For this reason, even if the feedback is the same, the two are different.
Code: Feedback only on the content of the description/Feedback on the content of the experience gained by the person

Text: The response was made after reading the student's long record. On the other hand, this is the same as the student's entry in No. 18, and as a result, the AI-generated feedback is probably similar in content. If a student repeats the same entry, a human being can focus on different points each time or give guidance on writing different content, but at least in this setting, it was possible to see that the AI was not good enough in this respect.
Code: Feedback in the same pattern

Text: This student has written about the recovery of a patient with impaired swallowing function with great emotion. I think that the comment, which asks the student to write specifically based on the other parts of the description without mentioning this student's excellent description, is somewhat off the point as feedback from the supervising doctor on the content of this student's record.
Code: Lack of feedback on the emotional aspects

Text: The two points for improvement are clearly indicated. On the other hand, although the AI feedback in general mentions the need for specificity, there is a concern as to whether it would be possible to provide appropriate feedback in response to specific learning issues, etc. if they were written in this record. It may be preferable to receive face-to-face guidance on issues and questions that arise in actual learning.
Code: Limits of feedback on the content of the learning

Text: It seems to be a coincidence, but in this case, the same points (the specific content of the description regarding BLS) are requested in both feedbacks. The description is more supportive.
Code: Feedback for making descriptions more concrete/Similar to human beings

Text: Good points: The improvement measures are specific. Points for improvement: The supportive messages may be a little excessive. They feel a little forced.
Code: Excessively supportive descriptions

Text: Good points: The advice is specific. The content doesn't feel too strange. Bad points: In some cases, asking for detailed patient lists could lead to the writing of personal information that is prohibited. This point should also be covered in the guidance.
Code: Advice that leads to the leakage of personal information

Text: The advice is specific. On the other hand, asking for the content of the patient's story could infringe on personal information, so care is needed with this comment.
Code: Advice that leads to the leakage of personal information

Text: The fact that the student is asking for a record on January 13th, a Saturday, without being aware of this is something that should be improved. The fact that the student is asking for improvements while giving examples of the actual content is easy to understand. However, it is important to remember that the real feedback needed for the student's training is not to be able to write well, but to actually learn.
Code: Advice that does not take the situation into account/Advice that focuses only on the content of the description

Text: The good points and areas for improvement are clearly indicated based on the student's content. However, the content of the advice is a little too general.
Code: Abstractness of advice

Text: Good points: Specific and with a lot of text. Points for improvement: The only advice given is “It would be good to write in more detail”.
Code: Large amount of text/Concrete advice/Advice that focuses only on the content of the description

Text: The fact that the points for improvement are detailed is commendable. On the other hand, the suggestions seem a little off the mark, such as “Try to cover all days” (I think this probably means to write a record every day), or “Read two documents to improve your presentation skills”, etc.
Code: Unnatural Japanese/Too high a goal

Text: There are also some entries that are not true, such as the fact that the diary for May 9th is blank, even though there is a record (albeit not a full one). The advice given for malignant thymoma also seems specific but is not very substantial. However, the comments are based on a careful reading of the student's record.
Code: Mix of descriptions that differ from the facts/Advice that lacks substance

Text: Improvement points: The only advice given is to write more specifically. Good points: The comments are based on the content.

Text: The feedback is polite and specific, and it suggests ways to solve the problem. The feedback on how to approach the review of principles is not very helpful.

Text: The feedback is specific. It is unclear whether the student is recording their daily activities, but the fact that they are recording them is highly evaluated.

Text: There is a little positive feedback at the beginning, but the corrective feedback is too detailed, and it ends up blurring the positive feedback. If you are going to write something so specific and corrective, I think it would be better to give specific examples of what is good.

Text: I think it is good that specific examples of what is good are given at the beginning. The logic breaks down when it says “I keep a record every day” but then says “there are days when there is no record”. Even if there are no problems with each individual segment, there is still a sense of discomfort when looking at the text as a whole.

Text: I also judge that this case does not have a daily record. When judging only from this log, I cannot understand the basis for judging that there is no daily record. I think this is a very good constructive feedback, as it is specific.

Text: It is in the form of feedback. However, I think that the student has described the treatment content and its effects in detail, but it is pointed out that they have not been described, and it is a little confusing when reading it.

Text: The reviewer points out that it would be better to write “Ask the patient about their lifestyle and eating habits in detail...” rather than “Ask about the patient's lifestyle history”, but when you think about it carefully, it's not really that big a deal. Text: I think it would be better to also specifically mention the positive points. There are quite a few good points, but I get the impression that they were glossed over. Text: I think that the positive feedback is well-targeted and will help to motivate the students. The constructive feedback is not well-targeted and is not very meaningful.

Text: I think it's a normal record for a student to say that they didn't do a good job with the medical interview, so they'll do better tomorrow, but you've pointed out that it should be more specific. Text: Good points: The specific points for improvement in the report are stated. The path for advancing learning is indicated. Points for improvement: The tone seems too high-handed. It seems like “great work” is being overstated, and it also seems like the resident is just writing what they need to in order to get the report submitted. It feels a little like they can't read the situation. And yet, it is giving feedback based on sound reasoning. If this is AI-generated, I felt that there might be a gap between the character of the supervising doctor in their normal life. There might be a mechanical sense of incongruity, an artificial “uncanny valley”-like sense of incongruity. Also, there might be a context that is shared between the supervising doctor and the resident, but is not included in the resident's report.
Code: Specific advice on the description/unnatural tone

Text: Good points: The report is being given feedback on the specific methods used to write it. The advice is to make the details of the experience more specific in order to deepen the learning from the practical training. Bad points: The focus seems to be on making the report look presentable. It is unreasonable to ask for more specific details on the 25th, which is written as an absence. The setting of using the example of a newborn patient to deepen understanding of the indications and management methods for CPAP is also unreasonable as an approach after the practical training has finished.
Code: Specific advice on the description/assistance with putting experiences into words/advice focusing only on the content of the description/too high a demand

Text: While supportive comments are made, the report is also pointed out for improvement in a precise and formative manner. On the other hand, it seems unrealistic and unnatural to praise the fact that the medical student has “carried out the practice record in a thorough manner” in the report. In reality, I think that feedback would be written based on the student's usual attitude during the practice, which is not included in the report.
Code: Specific advice on the description/advice focusing only on the content of the description

Text: The comment “Carefully record your daily activities” is a little over the top and seems mechanical, as it only refers to a single day's worth of activities. The feedback about writing more detailed and accurate descriptions of the practical training content is accurate. The points for improvement mention the attitude of the trainee.
Code: Inaccurate interpretation/lack of perspective on practical training attitude

Text: The instructor comments on the problems raised by the learner. On the other hand, the comment about the lack of a record of learning for 3/26-29 may be off the mark in some cases, as it may be that the day was not a training day as part of the curriculum, or that the learner simply did not mention that they were absent. The setting of the scene for explaining the patient's condition is also abstract, and I felt that the accuracy of the feedback would depend on whether the instructor and learner had experienced the same actual interview situation.
Code: Inaccurate interpretation/advice not taking the situation into account

Text: The feedback is supportive for the learner, who has only written about the practical matters. The report is accurately instructed on the content necessary to achieve the evaluation criteria. If this were an AI, there would be a difference in the tone of feedback2, and it might feel mechanical.

Text: Although the learner does not keep a daily record, the fact that they do keep a daily record is evaluated, and this feels strange. The feedback encourages the learner to think more deeply about the content of their daily practical work and to reflect on it. There is no mention of the issue of how to interact with patients, so feedback is needed.
Code: Inaccurate interpretation/assistance in concretizing experience/assistance in encouraging self-reflection based on experience/lack of reference to the content of the practical training

Text: The feedback seems mechanical, based on the records. The wording gives the impression of being a translation from English.

Text: The wording gives the impression of being translated, and seems unnatural. For example, “It's a good start.” The feedback is limited to how to record things. Although the aim is to record things every day, there is a gap between this and reality, such as during consecutive holidays, and this makes it unnatural. The patient list is written in detail, but I don't think it is written in particularly detailed fashion, so I feel a sense of incongruity.

Text: The wording feels like it has been translated. The way the foundation is created, the signposts, etc. There is good feedback on how to record and deepen the information. There is no feedback on the attitude of the practical training, so improvement is needed.

Text: The writing feels mechanical and like a translation. After mentioning the good points, there are some points that could be improved. Text: Although the feedback is based on the student's record, it is feedback that stands out for its inappropriate points in the details. This is an issue that is often mentioned in generated AI, but because it lacks common sense, it points out that “there are only four days of records” without taking into account the fact that January 8th is a public holiday. Also, when examining joints in the field of rheumatology, I think that the focus should be on the presence or absence of joint tenderness, spontaneous pain, or limited range of motion, rather than joint fluid retention, but the only example given for examining joints is “how to examine for joint fluid retention”, which is inappropriate feedback given the characteristics of the department.

Text: The point of showing specific examples to show the areas for improvement in the student's reflection is easy to understand.
Code: Advice with specific examples

Text: I think the suggestion that it would be good to decide on the teaching materials to be used and the study time as the goal for review is reasonable, but I think the expression “run MRI analysis” is a bit mechanical. Is it not possible to generate a phrase like “Learn about the normal images of head MRI and the imaging findings for each disease using ○○, and compare them with images of actual patients”?
Code: Unnatural Japanese

Text: I felt that the comments were very detailed and thorough. The feedback was mainly about how the students wrote their reports and the content of their reports, and I think it lacked feedback from the perspective of what kind of actions the students should take in future practical training.
Code: Polite advice on the content of the description/missing reference to behavior in the practical training

Text: I think that the feedback was given while giving appropriate specific examples about how to write practical training records.
Code: Presentation of specific examples to improve the description

Text: As it seems that there are only two days' worth of records, I feel that the feedback “It is very good that you are keeping a record every day” is not accurate. Along with the suggestion that the student's record should be written in more detail, the feedback also gives specific examples of what kind of perspective should be taken when writing the record, which I think will be helpful for the student.
Code: Inaccurate advice/presentation of specific examples

Text: I think that specific points are being made in line with the student's record.
Code: Specific points raised

Text: I think that the feedback is being given in line with the student's record and the standards for clinical practice.
Code: Strict adherence to standards

Text: I think it's good that the points to consider when making specific records are pointed out, along with specific examples. I'm curious about what the phrase “Please cheer up!” is based on, as it appears in the student's record.
Code: Advice that is not appropriate to the situation

Text: I think it's constructive that there is a specific action plan for keeping a record every day. I also think that specific points are made, such as advice on how to improve the record to make it more detailed, and how to set behavioral goals.
Code: Specific advice for setting goals

Text: I think it's good that the student is being pointed in the right direction about what kind of learning and recording is needed to make the future issues more concrete.
Code: Specific advice for setting goals

Text: The student's summary of the lecture, “In my opinion, the medical interview is important for muscular diseases”, is followed by the advice, “Please try to describe at what point you learned this”. However, I think this learning was gained from the lecture and not from being in front of a real patient, and the reason why the medical interview is important is also described, so I feel that the advice is a little imprecise.
Code: Inaccurate advice

Text: I think the point about setting specific behavioral goals related to future issues is appropriate, but I think it is a little excessive to demand specific behavioral goals for self-study, let alone for practical training. I don't think the student's record lacks specificity, so I feel that the feedback is slightly inaccurate.
Code: Too high a demand

Text: I think the point about using too many technical terms is accurate, but I think the point about demanding specific goal setting for the record “to be able to write medical records smoothly” is making a point of little importance.
Code: Setting of too high a priority for the content of the description

Text: Feedback is given in line with the record, and specific points are also raised. However, I think that the specific example given as an improvement for the entry “perform arterial blood collection techniques” - “increase opportunities to practice blood collection from specific arteries” - is a little unnatural.
Code: Advice that does not take into account medical knowledge

Text: Although the specific content of the practice is not mentioned, the points for revision regarding the record are detailed. It seems like the way to write the record will improve.
Code: Detailed advice on how to record

Text: I get the impression that it is a template. It seems like the way to write the record will improve.
Code: Routine advice/support for how to write records

Text: As it only points out the shortcomings and does not mention the content of the practical training, it gives the impression that the practical training was not observed.
Code: Excessive pointing out of shortcomings/lack of pointing out of practical training content

Text: I feel that the description of the good points is too abstract. Although the fact that there is a gap in time and the fact that there are omissions in the record are not the same thing, it feels like one-sided feedback, such as pointing out that there are omissions in the record or suggesting that some of the abstract writing should be corrected.
Code: Lack of specificity in pointing out good points/excessive pointing out of areas for improvement

Text: It feels like the content was not observed, as it points out that the daily record should be continued for each day's record.
Code: Only pointing out shortcomings

Text: I feel that the feedback is not focused on the way the record is written, and is not being used to improve the content of the practical training or learning.
Code: Advice focusing only on how to write/lack of reference to practical training content

Text: The feedback that points out specific ways of writing is too objective, so I feel that it would be better if it was in the form of questions or assignments, as that would motivate me to do more research.
Code: Too objective advice

Text: The points that are praised are too abstract, and most of the content is directive. It would be better if there was advice on the content related to the practical training, rather than on how to write the record.
Code: Vague pointing out of good points/lack of reference to practical training content

Text: I felt that the feedback would be more useful if it included advice on how to learn and what to do in the future.
Code: Making future issues more concrete

Text: The entire feedback only refers to the criteria for the record, and the suggested action plan is too difficult.
Code: Too high a demand for goals

Text: Not only does the feedback only refer to the criteria for the record, but it also points out that the patient list is missing from the record on February 2nd, even though it is not supposed to be included, and I feel that this is inappropriate feedback.
Code: Advice not taking the situation into account/insistence on standards alone

Text: For a single day's worth of records, it gives a false evaluation that “the record is not being done every day”.

The feedback was given based on the criteria for recording, even though the content of the record, which is a case summary, is difficult to evaluate. I felt that the feedback about the need to record changes in treatment would be useful for future practice.
Code: Specific advice on the content of the description

Text: It was difficult to understand whether adding stage II as supplementary information would increase understanding. As the feedback was mainly about the record, I felt that it would be better if there was feedback about the behavior during the practice.
Code: Lack of reference to behavior in practical training/advice that is not appropriate to the situation

Text: I felt that the feedback was given in a way that praised the record, but also emphasized the need to meet the criteria for the record, even though the record was thin on content. I think it would be better if the suggestions for revision were not given in specific sentences.
Code: Advice with specific examples/unfavorable reaction to specific examples

Text: The feedback emphasizes the points that do not meet the criteria for the record. I think it would be better if the content that meets the criteria was also mentioned in more official terms.
Code: Strict adherence to standards/lack of positive support

Text: My impression is that the feedback is given after the record has been carefully examined. The fact that the points that should be evaluated and the good points are mentioned first, and then the points for improvement are pointed out, makes it easier for the student to accept the points for improvement, and I think that is a good thing. I also think that the points for improvement and the content of the points for improvement are very specific, and that makes it easier to take action. The main points for improvement are suggestions for how the students' practical training should be carried out, and there is no feedback on medical or clinical content.
Code: Lack of reference to medical content/advice based on records/suggestions for improvements that are easy to accept

Text: The main points are descriptions of the students' learning attitudes and methods during practical training. This is thought to lead to improvements in the way the students approach practical training and improvements in learning efficiency, but there seems to be a lack of feedback from a medical or clinical perspective.
Code: Advice on learning methods/lack of medical perspective

Text: There are many words of praise for the students, and this is good as it is used effectively. As points for improvement, it would be better if there was feedback from a clinical perspective.
Code: Use of positive language/lack of medical perspective

Text: The fact that it gives words of encouragement to the students is good. The fact that it explains how to record and how to write in depth using specific examples is good.
Code: Use of positive language/suggestions for improvements with specific examples

Text: The feedback is based on the students' records. Basically, the feedback is intended to improve the students' learning by improving how they write in their records. It would be good to have feedback and knowledge presented from a clinical perspective, as well as learning advice.
Code: Advice based only on the content of the record/Lack of medical perspective

Text: The evaluation is based on the “criteria” required of the students. It seems that there is a lack of feedback from a clinical perspective.
Code: Strict adherence to standards/Lack of medical perspective

Text: The impression was that feedback was being given based on the “criteria” required of students. The main points were comments on how to fill in the student records, and there was a lack of clinical feedback.
Code: Strict adherence to standards/Lack of medical perspective

Text: The main points were comments on how to fill in the student records. The comments were based on the “criteria”. There was a lack of comments from a clinical knowledge or perspective.
Code: Advice based only on the content of the record/Strict adherence to standards/Lack of medical perspective

Text: The main points were comments on how to fill in the student records. The aim is to improve students' learning through improving how to fill in the records. There were words of encouragement, and it was friendly.
Code: Use of positive language/Friendliness through the use of positive language

Text: The main points of the feedback are related to how to record student records. The feedback is given based on the “criteria” required of students. It is good that the points to be evaluated are clearly indicated.
Code: Clarification of evaluation points/Strict adherence to standards/Suggestions for improvements in the way records are taken

Text: The student's learning is encouraged by improving the content of the student records. The feedback is given based on the content of the student records, so it would be good if there was also some presentation of directly related medical knowledge and viewpoints.
Code: Suggestions for improvements in how records are kept/advice based solely on the content of the records/lack of a medical perspective

Text: The main points of the feedback are related to improving the content of the student records and encouraging learning through this. It would be good if there were also specific clinical content and knowledge presented, as well as content that provides guidelines.
Code: Advice based solely on the content of the records/lack of a medical perspective

Text: The main focus is on improving the student records and providing feedback to encourage learning through this. It was good that specific examples were given to show areas for improvement. It would be good to add specific clinical content, knowledge, and guidance.
Code: Advice based solely on the content of the records/lack of a medical perspective

Text: The main focus is on improving the student records and providing feedback to encourage learning through this. It was good that the student's good points were clearly shown and evaluated. There is a lack of specific clinical content, knowledge, and guidance.
Code: Advice based solely on the content of the records/lack of a medical perspective

Text: The evaluation is based on the “criteria”. The main point is to improve the student records. It is good that it shows the points for improvement using specific examples. Although it encourages the description of clinical aspects, it seems that there is a lack of direct clinical knowledge and clues in terms of learning clinical medicine.
Code: Strict adherence to standards/suggestions for improvements in how records are kept/presentation of specific examples

Text: I felt that the evaluation was based on “criteria”. It was good that the good points were clearly indicated. It was good that the learning effect was improved by encouraging students to write specific descriptions even for terms that they were just listing. Overall, I got the impression that it was a form of encouraging students to learn through improving the way they write in their student records.
Code: Clear identification of good points/assistance with making records more concrete/presentation of specific examples

Text: The advice is clear and easy to understand
Code: Clear advice

Text: The suggestions are a little difficult to understand. I would like you to clearly indicate specific examples.
Code: Vague advice

Text: It would be even better if there were descriptions of what improving the level of detail and the concreteness of what you have learned leads to
Code: Focusing only on the detail of the record

Text: The specific examples lack concreteness
Code: Lack of concreteness

Text: More suggestions tailored to each situation are needed
Code: Suggestions that do not take the situation into account

Text: The level of specificity is lacking
The order of priority is wrong
The suggestions are broadly outlined
The writing is polite
It is difficult to read
The specific examples given are ideas that are not based on much medical expertise
Code: Advice that is not based on medical knowledge

Text: It would be better if the future benefits of the suggestions were also shown, along with the feedback
Code: Insistence on only the standards

Text: The suggestion to focus on newborn care as a specific goal is also rather abstract
I think it lacks specificity
I think the specific examples of the goals are weak
It is easy to understand

Expressions such as “Great job!” and “I will provide feedback.” are English-Japanese translation-like expressions, and I felt that they were unnatural as Japanese. Also, “Read five papers related to hyponatremia” is a very interesting suggestion, as it is like a lab duty in the sciences, such as “read 100 papers a week.”
Code: Unnatural Japanese/Suggesting specific goals using numerical values

Text: This is feedback that recommends specifically describing things, and I think it is good feedback for students who were not aware of these things.
Code: Recommending the detail of the record

Text: I thought it was wonderful that the beginning of the feedback praised the effort made to fill in the record items. I felt that having this one process made it easier to listen to specific improvement measures. Also, all of them are feasible.
Code: Pointing out what is being done well

Text: The point that the feedback was given under the misunderstanding that the daily record was being done when it was not should be improved. Other than that, there was a lot of practical feedback.
Code: Inaccurate advice/Advice that is easy to put into practice

Text: I was surprised that such detailed feedback could be given regarding the setting of the place for explaining the patient's condition.
Code: Detailed advice with specific examples

Text: It seems as if they are fixated on just filling in the blanks. If the student is not aiming to become a surgeon, they may not feel the significance of recording the techniques they learn in surgery, not to mention their knowledge of anatomy.
Code: Insistence on only the standards

Text: I felt that the feedback was somewhat verbose and required detailed descriptions. Of course, if you read it carefully, you will understand, but I think that students would be happier with feedback that they can understand the main points of at a glance.
Code: Redundant expressions

Text: I think it's a very good teaching method to first give praise and then describe areas for improvement. However, there are two points for improvement for every point of praise, so the person reading it might lose interest a little.
Code: Too many points for improvement

Text: I thought it was amazing that the AI would write “It would be good to be more specific about how to increase nutrition, such as by adding milk”. It felt like something a detailed pediatrician teaching doctor might say.
Code: Assistance with making the experience record more concrete

Text: I don't know if there is a need for medical students to record the specific procedures for surgery.
Code: Advice that is not suited to the situation

Text: I wonder if it would be meaningful for the student to be given detailed feedback on the content of the slides they are creating... (Even after a year, the student will remember the content of the slides they created in that department, and even if they do remember it, it is questionable whether it would be beneficial.)
Code: Advice that is not suited to the situation

Text: I think it is inevitable that the record will have few entries. However, I wonder whether the student would want to take on board this long-winded feedback, which is generally lacking in praise, and change their approach.
Code: Excessive points for improvement for content that is not well described

Text: I felt that the list of pointed comments was lacking in supportive words for the student.
Code: Excessive points for improvement

Text: The content was easy to understand because it was divided into three sections and each section had a title.
Code: Structured advice

Text: I felt that it would be easier to read if the lines were broken appropriately.
Code: Long sentences with no line breaks

Text: Overall, I thought it was very useful because it promoted concretization. In addition, it praised the fact that they were keeping a record every day.
Code: Points for improvement/promoting the concretization of records

Text: I felt the feedback was too short. The good points are that it mentions the content of the description, so you can tell that it has been read and written. The points that need to be improved are that it does not mention all the content of the description, and the feedback feels insufficient.
Code: Short description/neglect of content

Text: The feedback seems very short. The good points are that it reads the content of the medical student's description. The points that need to be improved are that the only content is ‘I will teach you in the future’, and I feel that it is not content that will improve the learning motivation of the medical student who wrote the description.
Code: Short description/neglect of content/pointing out areas for improvement Missing

Text: It felt like feedback from a doctor who was examining the patient together. The good points were that it covered the transition from the acute phase to the chronic phase, and it also described what kind of learning would be desirable next, based on how the transition would progress in the future. The points that needed to be improved were that there was no feedback on the ‘gas exchange’ and ‘blood sampling in newborns’ that had been learned, although there was a description of them.
Code: Record based on sharing experiences/advice based on clinical situation/pointing out areas for improvement Missing

Text: It was concise and easy to understand. The good points were that the feedback answered the questions that the medical students had raised as issues for the future, and that it showed support for learning by mentioning that learning anatomy would be useful for observing surgery in the future. There were no points that needed to be improved.
Code: Answering medical questions/showing support for learning

Text: It was clear that the content was based on the evaluation criteria and that the medical students' own attitude towards the practical training was also being observed, not just the record. One good point was that it mentioned the need for independent study in response to the issues that the medical students had raised as issues for the future. As for areas for improvement, I felt that the feedback was lacking in specificity for the medical students' future studies.
Code: Description based on observations in practical training/lack of specificity

Text: I feel that it was kind feedback. As for good points, it praised the students and suggested where they should study next. There are no areas for improvement.
Code: Specific advice based on medical knowledge

Text: It praised the fact that the practical training was fulfilling. As for good points, it praised the students for their proactive training. As for areas for improvement, I felt that there were no specific examples given.
Code: Missing specific examples of improvement

Text: The content conveys the feeling of working together with medical students. The good points are the same as above. There are no points for improvement.
Code: Feedback that appeals to emotions/feedback based on shared experiences

Text: The fact that the medical students were participating in the training is praised, even though there is little mention of them. The good points are that the medical students are praised. There are no points for improvement.
Code: Description based on behaviour in practical training

Text: The feedback is short in relation to the medical students‘ entries. The good points are that the medical students’ independence is being developed. The points for improvement are that I think the motivation would increase if feedback was given on what they had learnt and what their future challenges are.
Code: Neglect of content/short description

Text: The impression is that the medical students' records are not being touched upon. The good point is that it mentions participation in practical training. The point for improvement is that it does not mention anything about what they have learnt or what they are working on, so it is not really feedback.
Code: Neglect of content/evaluation based on observations during the practical training/lack of suggestions for improvement

Text: The impression is that it is commenting on the part where they have written about future issues. The good point is that it replies to the content that the medical students have written about as future issues and what they have learnt, and also replies about how they can learn more and about setting up future opportunities. There are no points for improvement.
Code: Assurance of reflection in practical training

Text: There was feedback not only on the content of the written report, but also on the content of the presentation during the practical training, and it was clear that the attitude of the student during the practical training was being observed. As a positive point, it was noted that the content of the presentation was well-summarised regarding the risk classification of paediatric leukaemia. As a point for improvement, it was noted that there was no mention of what other areas of learning would be useful for the next stage of learning.
Code: Description based on observations during the practical training/description of specific good points based on medical knowledge/lack of suggestions for improvement

Text: There was no written comment on the content of the written report by the medical student. As a positive point, it was noted that the content of the written report was being read. As a point for improvement, it was noted that there was no feedback on the content of the written report.
Code: Neglect of content

Text: The content shows that the students are being supported in their learning and practical training. The good points are that the content not only refers to what was written, but also mentions the students‘ attitude during the practical training and the content of their presentations. The improvement point is that there was no comment on the nephrotic syndrome, which was written about as something that had been learned.
Code: Description based on observations during the practical training/neglect of content

Text: The content shows that the students’ written content was read and commented on. The good points are that the content written about was not only corrected where the learners had made mistakes, but also included details that would lead to further learning. There are no improvement points.
Code: Pointing out errors related to medical knowledge/encouragement to learn more

Text: The impression is that it not only covers the content of the description, but also the student's attitude during the practical training. The good points are that it describes what was good about the process from preparing for the case presentation to the actual presentation, and I felt that it was content that would increase the student's motivation for further study. There are no particular points for improvement.
Code: Description touching on attitude during practical training

Text: (Good points) The feedback is based on an awareness of the actual patient's condition and the experience that goes with it. (Points for improvement) The amount of description is small. The content of the suggestions is a little imprecise.
Code: Descriptions that are conscious of the experience gained during the practical training/short descriptions/lack of specificity in the suggestions

Text: (Good points) It is clear that the comments are based on the actual experience of the student, not just on the content of the student's description. It is supportive content for the student. (Bad points) The comments and advice are about the student's training itself, and there is little mention of the content of the record. However, I think this is unavoidable, as it is thought that this feedback from the supervising doctor is more about the training itself than about the content of the record. In fact, it is more important to give feedback on the training itself than on the content of the record.
Code: Descriptions based on the experience gained during the practical training/neglect of the content of the description

Text: The supportive aspects are good, but it's just too short. However, if the supervising doctor and student are communicating well on the spot, then this is not a problem.
Code: Descriptions that are too short

Text: The student has written a long piece of text, but the feedback is brief, and I think the student may be worried that the supervising doctor hasn't read it even though they wrote it so carefully and at such length. On the other hand, it is a good point that it mentions learning in conjunction with matters that are of great interest to students, such as the national exam.
Code: Descriptions that are in line with the student's interests/descriptions that are too short

Text: Rather than feedback on the student's notes or practical training content, the content is more focused on recruiting students to the department. On the other hand, from the student's point of view, there is a good chance that the content will be more memorable than the usual feedback. If we consider that the role of the supervising doctor is not only to encourage learning, but also to provide role models and information on detailed career paths, then this feedback may have an important message.
Code: Descriptions that are conscious of recruitment

Text: The content gives students a sense of security, as it tells them about the schedule for future practical training in the form of ‘follow-up with a mini-lecture’.
Code: Expressions of intent to reflect on the practical training/interaction with the practical training

Text: The message is clear about what the student should learn next. It contains a lot of specific support content.
Code: Specific presentation of the content to be studied

Text: It seems to be a coincidence, but both pieces of feedback this time asked for almost the same points (the specific content of the description about BLS). Because it is a particular hospital, and we have not been able to share direct experience, commenting based only on the student's record may not be that different between humans and AI. Although the comments are based on the evaluation criteria, the student may feel that they are lacking in flavour.
Code: Similar to AI due to a lack of direct experience sharing

Text: Although it is a short comment, it does mention the good points.
Code: Short descriptions/descriptions of good points

Text: Good points: The feedback focuses on the student's future learning content. Bad points: There are no comments on the student's previous experience or records.
Code: Presentation of future learning behaviour

Text: Although it is a supportive comment, it is not very substantial.
Code: Lack of elements as feedback

Text: Good points: It indicates areas for improvement to some extent. Bad points: I think there are not many comments on the student's experience.
Code: Lack of description of experience/description based only on what is written

Text: This is partly because the student's record content is limited to listing the names of surgeries and lacks specificity, but it does not seem that the comments are based on the content of the record. It is short.
Code: Disregard for what is written/lack of comments on what is written

Text: Good points: The fact that the medical record is evaluated and that communication with residents and others is evaluated is clearly conveyed. Points for improvement: It is too short.
Code: Evaluation based on behaviour in practical training/description too short

Text: Although it is short, it does include both good points and points for improvement. It is a comment on the feedback itself, and there is not much comment on the content of the student's experience.
Code: Description based only on what is written

Text: The student's record is also simple, so it is not possible to know the results, but it is too short.
Code: Description too short

Text: Good points: It indicates the student's next task. Bad points: It is not clear which part is meant by ‘appropriate assessment’.
Code: Unclear points for improvement

Text: It encourages students to be motivated and proactive. There is no specific feedback.
Code: Lack of specificity in points for improvement

Text: It provides information that will be needed in the future. However, it does not provide technical feedback.
Code: Clear points for observation in practical training

Text: It points out errors in writing and correctly teaches medical knowledge. It does not provide specific suggestions for improvement.
Code: Pointing out medical errors/lack of specific points for improvement

Text: It is clear that it will be implemented, but there are no suggestions for how to improve it.
Code: Lack of specific points for improvement

Text: It conveys what can be learned in neurology and how it will be useful in the future. There is no feedback on what was done in particular.
Code: Presentation of future learning policy

Text: The learner's behaviour is evaluated.
Code: Description based on experience in practical training

Text: Feedback is given on the content of the practical work other than what is written.
Code: Descriptions based on observations made during practical training

Text: As the learner has written a lot about anatomical structures, the feedback encourages them to learn while empathising with the complexity of the structures. However, there is no specific feedback, and it is too brief.
Code: Empathy towards the learner

Text: The practical work is summarised from a bird's eye view, and the evaluator's interest is conveyed while encouraging the learner to verbalise.
Code: Encouraging the learner to verbalise their experiences

Text: Advice is given to encourage active participation. There is no evaluation at all of what was done.
Code: Presentation of learning policy/ lack of description of the content implemented

Text: The name is written, so you know who gave the feedback. No specific suggestions for improvement are given.
Code: Descriptions linked to people/ lack of specific points for improvement

Text: The good points that were actually observed are evaluated. No suggestions for improvement are given.
Code: Descriptions based on observations made during practical training/ lack of points for improvement

Text: The description is evaluated, but no specific suggestions for improvement are given.
Code: Descriptions based only on the content described/ lack of points for improvement

Text: No specific situation is evaluated, and no suggestions for improvement are given.
Code: Descriptions based only on the content described/ lack of points for improvement

Text: This is not feedback on the record, but a message about the practical training.
Code: Disregard for the content described

Text: It is a personal message with no specific suggestions for improvement.
Code: Lack of points for improvement

Text: The things that were not done are evaluated, and suggestions for improvement are given, but there is no specific mention of the things that were done well.
Code: Lack of pointing out what is good/Only pointing out areas for improvement

Text: As there is no mention of specific points for improvement, this is not feedback that encourages the other person to change.
Code: Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: This is not feedback. However, it does serve as a comment to reinforce the importance of what the student has learned.
Code: Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: This is not feedback. There is no positive feedback at all, and even as constructive feedback, it is not specific, such as ‘ask various questions’.
Code: Unclear description

Text: This is not feedback. Even if the student reads this, they will not change for the better or for the worse.
Code: Insufficient description

Text: There is positive feedback, but none that is constructive. Please keep up the good work. This motivates the student, but it does not lead to improvement.
Code: Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: This is not feedback, but the evaluator's response and thoughts.
Code: Insufficient description

Text: This is specific feedback that is substantial in content. However, I think that the student would also like feedback that reinforces the points they have done well. Also, there are many items, there is no priority, and it seems a little harsh.
Code: Too many points for improvement

Text: It is supportive, but not constructive.
Code: Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: There are no specific points for improvement. It is supportive, but there are no specific points to encourage growth.
Code: Only supportive content/Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: On the contrary, I think it is good feedback that helps students to deepen their learning by providing detailed feedback on their descriptions. However, in light of the assessment criteria, I think it should point out that there are few records for other days, but it does not do so.
Code: Deepening the description medically/Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: This is feedback that lacks concreteness. It may give an impression of the overall practical training, but it does not provide specific points for improvement.
Code: Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: This is feedback that lacks concreteness and is unable to encourage specific growth.
Code: Lack of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: The points that are being raised are detailed and specific, but I think that feedback that covers the whole is necessary.
Code: Detailed description of the content of the practical training/neglect of the content of the description

Text: The good points of the student are mentioned, but the good points of the record are not mentioned. However, this is something that cannot be judged from the record (so it is clear who it is), so it is very good as feedback, but it is difficult to evaluate as feedback purely from the record.
Code: Evaluation based on observations during the practical training/difficulty in applying to the evaluation criteria

Text: It is thought that this is not feedback taken out of the record, so it is impossible to evaluate. Therefore, I have given it all a score of 1.
There is no specific, constructive feedback, and while you can strengthen what you can do, there is no indication of the points that you need to be able to do what you can't.
Code: Lack of presentation of points for improvement

Text: I can understand what the assessor is trying to say, but this is not a comprehensive record feedback.
Code: Neglect of the content of the description

Text: There are hardly any records written by the medical students themselves, so it is impossible to assess them. I suspect that the feedback was written by the supervising doctors who were observing the students, so I think they probably didn't focus on giving verbal feedback. I think it would have been good to encourage the medical students to keep a record of their learning every day, or at least to write down the content and results of their learning in detail, so that they could look back on it later.
Code: Lack of pointing out of shortcomings/lack of presentation of points for improvement

Text: I think it would be good to indicate the best points and one point for improvement.
Code: Lack of pointing out of good points and points for improvement

Text: I think the feedback is very good because the points for improvement are specific and not too many.
Code: Pointing out specific points for improvement

Text: I think it would be good to point out even just one point for improvement for the medical student.
Code: Lack of presentation of points for improvement

Text: I think it is good feedback that emphasises the importance of the student's initiative and a supportive attitude, but I think it would be good to also point out one specific goal or point for improvement.
Code: Expression of a supportive attitude/lack of presentation of points for improvement

Text: I think it would be good to point out one good point and one point for improvement for the medical student.
Code: Pointing out specific points for improvement

Text: I think this is good feedback, as it gives specific advice on the items that the medical student should focus on learning, based on the medical student's record.
Code: Medical advice based on the record

Text: I think it is very good that the expert answers the questions the medical student has learned, but I think it would be even better if the medical student was given one specific point to improve on.
Code: Advice from the perspective of an expert

Text: It is difficult to evaluate this because there are almost no records of the medical student, but I suspect that this is probably feedback from the supervising doctor who was watching the student during the practical training. I think that it would be easier to reflect on the feedback and make it better if it included even just a few specific words about the feedback given verbally.
Code: Lack of indication of areas for improvement

Text: I think it would be better if the supervising doctor included even just one point about the medical student's areas of improvement or suggestions for improvement.
Code: Lack of indication of good points and areas for improvement

Text: I think this is good feedback because it points out areas for improvement while also being supportive of the student.
Code: Indication of areas for improvement

Text: The advice is concise and easy to take on board. I think it would be better if you could also mention one good point about the medical student.
Code: Lack of indication of good points/advice that is easy to put into practice

Text: The feedback also mentions the medical student's career path and future, so I think it shows that the supervising doctor is communicating well with the medical student. I think it would be better if you could also point out one improvement point.
Code: Description based on communication during the practical training/Lack of indication of areas for improvement

Text: The good points about the medical student are shown to a certain extent in concrete terms, which is good, but I think the feedback would be even better if you could also point out one improvement point in concrete terms.
Code: Indication of good points/Lack of indication of areas for improvement

Text: It felt like the feedback of a supervising doctor who places importance on verbal feedback in the workplace. I think it would be better if the good points and areas for improvement were listed specifically, even if only one at a time, so that the medical student can easily look back on them later.
Code: Lack of indication of good points and areas for improvement

Text: The feedback touched on the characteristics of the medical student and praised them, so I felt it was the feedback of a supervising doctor who had observed the student closely. I think the advice was also specific and good.
Code: Description based on observations during the practical training/Specific advice

Text: Although it was not very long and not very specific, I think it was encouraging feedback.
Code: Short description/Abstract description/Motivational description

Text: Although the amount is not large and it is a little abstract, it describes the actual content of the practical training that is not written in the log, and I think it is feedback that makes the student feel ‘they are watching me properly’ and is therefore very encouraging.
Code: Description based on the content of the practical training

Text: The point of the feedback being about how to write the log is the same as feedback1, but the point of trying to cultivate metacognition makes it easier to understand ‘why you should write concretely’ (however, I think the word ‘metacognition’ is difficult for students).
Code: Presentation of the significance of the description

Text: The feedback is tailored to the student's experience and based on actual cases. It's not feedback that is tailored to a standard, but it feels like ‘living’ feedback that is likely to bring about behavioural change.
Code: Descriptions that match the experience of the practical training/non-conformance with standards

Text: Although the feedback is about the criteria for how to write the log, the word ‘in practice’ is used, which I think makes it feel more realistic. Also, the word ‘final week’ makes you think about the overall summary of the whole thing
Code: Presentation of the significance of the description

Text: Good luck with the practical training! is a supportive comment, but I don't think it's feedback
Code: Words of encouragement/presentation of points for improvement Missing

Text: Although there are only a few, I think the feedback is easy to understand as it proposes issues related to important and impressive content.
Code: Addressing important medical issues

Text: It is supportive and provides feedback on the learner's own actions, but it doesn't seem to be very specific guidance.
Code: Abstract descriptions

Text: It is supportive and provides feedback based on specific actions. I think the student would be happy to receive this kind of feedback. If I were to make a point for improvement, it would be that not all of the content is provided in the feedback.
Code: Supportive words/descriptions based on the words of the practical training

Text: I don't think a simple ‘Thank you for your hard work’ would be considered feedback.
Code: Descriptions that are too short

Text: The feedback is more supportive, as it is based on the student's behaviour during the practical training, rather than on the contents of the portfolio.
Code: Disregard for the content of the description/descriptions based on observations made during the practical training

Text: The feedback is not specific enough, as it does not say what is ‘not a problem’.
Code: Abstract descriptions

Text: The feedback is specific and based on the student's behaviour during the practical training. There are some parts that do not necessarily match the contents of the portfolio.
Code: Disregard for the content of the description/descriptions based on observations made during the practical training/specific descriptions

Text: The feedback is more supportive and useful for the learner, as it is based on the student's attitude during the practical training, rather than on the contents of the portfolio.
Code: Descriptions based on observations made during the practical training

Text: Although the portfolio is hardly mentioned, the FB is based on the student's attitude during the practical training and the content observed.
Code: Disregard for the content of the description/descriptions based on observations made during the practical training

Text: The FB focuses on blood collection, but it is more about encouraging experience than specific content.
Code: Descriptions that encourage experience

Text: ・The presentation was said to be educational, but there was no mention of how or what was learned, and the student's good points were not directly conveyed. ・The next step, which is to develop the habit of thinking carefully about each example, is difficult to measure. However, focusing on one next step may be good in terms of prioritisation.
Code: Abstract descriptions

Text: The phrase ‘one more push’ suggests that the practical training is seen as a painful or negative experience, and it also undermines the value of the instructor as someone who provides a place for learning.
Code: Inhibition of learning due to the expression of implicit assumptions

Text: I have no idea what is important. I don't understand the context of drawing illustrations of surgical findings, which is also affected by the fact that the student's description is lacking.
Code: Lack of description of the situation

Text: The fact that it is a difficult case for diagnosis may be impressive for the evaluator, but it does not contribute to the student's learning. As this is the only thing written, it is considered unsuitable for feedback.
Code: Priority given to the interests of the person writing the description

Text: It is excellent in terms of supportive language. It would be even better if it included specific points of good practice and areas for improvement.
Code: Supportive expressions/omission of pointing out good points and areas for improvement

Text: The feedback is limited to the content of the afternoon practical session on the final day. The feedback also lacks specificity (although it may have been given during the practical session, this cannot be ascertained from the text). It would be good if the same feedback could be given for other practical sessions.
Code: Abstract descriptions

Text: I felt that it would be beneficial to encourage learners to learn how to match up their own examination findings with the findings of the examination. The contrast between the learners' findings and the instructor's findings is also thought to have been made in the same context, but it seems to show the superiority and infallibility of the instructor, and I think that this is feedback that makes learners feel an authority gradient.
Code: Prompting action in practical training/introduction of an authority gradient

Text: There is no mention of the content of the practical training or the learner's own description. It is impossible to know what is not written, so there is a possibility that important feedback is being given without being written down, but I think it would be good to make sure that important feedback points are recorded.
Code: Disregard for the content of the description

Text: It is understandable that there are few opportunities to do peripheral nerve examinations, but I wonder whether this is something that all medical students should learn. I think that feedback that emphasises this point has problems in terms of prioritisation. It is hoped that appropriate learning goals will be set for medical students.
Code: Descriptions that are too focused on the interests of the expert

Text: The positive evaluation of attitude is emphasised. This is a point that is probably difficult for the learner to describe, but if it is based on direct observation, it is a very important point. There is little specific feedback, but this feedback can be seen as a result of focusing on the key points.
Code: Descriptions based on observations in practical training

Text: The supportive comments are good, but there is no specific feedback on the practical training. At least one of the learning objectives should be mentioned.
Code: Omission of pointing out areas for improvement

Text: The points raised are good, but it would be easier for the learner to understand if the feedback referred to more specific content.
Code: Abstract descriptions

Text: The comments are based on the student's description, so the feedback is accurate. It only addresses whether the knowledge is correct or not, and does not indicate what action should be taken next. It would be better if the next action could be indicated with an awareness of the learning process.
Code: Pointing out errors in medical knowledge/lack of indication of next action

Text: The student's description itself is concerned with the everyday details of the case (fever follow-up) and less important technical points (the speed of spinal fluid output), so I think this is a difficult case for feedback. In cases like this, instead of giving bland feedback that ignores the content of the description, it would be better if the feedback could point out the appropriate learning points for the specialist field.
Code: Neglect of content of description

Text: The evaluator's attitude, which seems to regard the case of a common presentation of a common disease as unimportant, is suggested by the text, and I am concerned about the impact this will have on the medical students. It would be desirable for the evaluator and the medical student to share in a clear way the goals that the medical student should aim for.
Code: Description too focused on specialist interest

Text: Compared to feedback1, this is brief and less detailed. There are few comments praising the good points. On the other hand, it mentions the limitations and the boundaries of the evaluation tool, which are unlikely to be covered in textbooks for students, and I think it is useful information from the perspective of practical clinical training. Perhaps it is a quiet supervisor who only tells you the important things.
Code: Lack of indication of points that have been done well/description from specialist perspective

Text: Compared to feedback1, there is no feedback in the practical training record. On the other hand, there is follow-up on the student's attitude during the practical training and the fact that they were unable to give a good presentation, and the fact that they made an effort to improve the content of their presentation is praised, so it feels like feedback that has looked closely at the practical training as a whole.
Code: Neglect of content of description/description based on observations made during practical training

Text: It gives the impression that the busy supervising doctor quickly wrote down their impressions of the student. They probably evaluated the student as having met the required standard with no particular problems when compared to the behavioural standards, but they did not evaluate the record that was written. It seems to be just a summary evaluation conclusion, so it is not thought to contribute to improving skills and attitude.
Code: Neglect of content of description

Text: They did not point out the mistakes in the words ‘irregular pulse’ and ‘Holter electrocardiogram’. I think it is encouraging for the student to receive feedback that is relevant to the actual content of the practical training. Although you have included a summary of the characteristics of the case study, I think you should give a little more feedback on this.
Code: Overlooking medical errors/description in line with practical training content

Text: You have not pointed out the mistake of ‘resuscitation termination’. Although you have mentioned the limitations and shortcomings of the instructor, and this is not content that will directly lead to the improvement of the learner's ability, I think that this is a description that will be well received by the learner. I think that this is a description that conveys ‘encouragement from senior doctors’.
Code: Overlooking medical errors/indication of limitations of teaching staff

Text: It is too businesslike and directive. I wonder if there was anything else you wanted to convey? I wonder if there was anything you wanted to praise?
Code: Description too short

Text: While it lacks the perspective of feedback1, it conveys positive evaluations of the student's attitude during the practical training, and also includes supportive words that will help the student in the future. When reading the two together, it feels like they make a good piece of feedback as a whole.
Code: Pointing out attitude during practical training

Text: It conveys that the instructor has given a positive evaluation of the practical training as a whole, but I think that's all it says. There is no mention of specific issues or points of achievement.
Code: Lack of indication of points for improvement

Text: I thought that it would be good feedback for a good student to be told that they were generally satisfied with the content of the practical training, and then to be given the next task from the perspective of skills and attitude, rather than knowledge.
Code: Suggestions for improvement in terms of attitude during practical training

Text: It is a positive evaluation, but it is too general and does not point out the next task (for better or worse).
Code: Abstract description/missing points for improvement

Text: In terms of referring to the evaluation criteria, it is inferior to feedback 1, but it is more in line with the content of the practical training and presents the next task, so I felt that the quality of the feedback as a whole was better here.
Code: Non-compliance with evaluation criteria/description in line with practical training situation

Text: The quantity and quality of the records seems to be lacking, but there is no comment on this. There are no comments on the content of the records.
Code: Neglect of content/missing points for improvement

Text: The student's records are almost the same as those of ID: 128, but the comments in the feedback2 record are almost the same as those in the feedback2 record for ID: 128. (Unless there was a processing error that resulted in the same record being duplicated and made into two records), is it not the case that the comments do not point out that the records of the other two students are almost the same, including their faults?
There are supportive comments. That's all. There are words asking for a report, but it's not clear what the person wants to be reported in addition.
Code: Abstract points for improvement

Text: It's too simple and doesn't amount to feedback.
Code: Too short a description

Text: I think it's good that the comments are supportive and refer to clinical difficulties and the ease of making mistakes. I thought it would be good to delve a little deeper into the record, which ends with the single phrase ‘neurological examination’.
Code: Opinion from a clinical perspective

Text: The part where the student is encouraged
Code: Description that encourages students

Text: The part where the student is encouraged.
Code: Description that encourages students

Text: The fact that the points of the medical examination are clearly stated suggests that the student will be able to take a more interested approach to the practical training.
Code: Clear indication of points to be observed during practical training

Text: I felt that this supervising doctor had a good grasp of the content of the students' practical training. It's good that specific points for improvement are listed, but I think it would be better if the importance and priority were also stated.
Code: Description based on practical training/missing prioritisation

Text: I think that feedback on the specific actions of the students is a great opportunity for them to become highly motivated. I don't think there are any particular points for improvement.
Code: Descriptions based on observations made during practical training/motivating students for practical training

Text: I think it would be better if the lack of daily records was noted. Other than that, I didn't notice anything in particular.
Code: Missing points for improvement/missing points for description

Text: I think it's great that the student's efforts are being recognised. I think it would be better if there were more specific instructions.
Code: Missing points for improvement

Text: I think the feedback shows consideration for the student's motivation. I think it would be better if there were comments on how to write the portfolio.
Code: Missing points for description

Text: I think the feedback doesn't discourage the student. I don't think there is anything more that could be said to this student.
I think it's great that the student is being encouraged. I don't think there is anything that needs to be improved.
Code: Descriptions to encourage students

Text: As an evaluation by many supervisors, I can relate to the content of this. As the student has written quite specific details, I thought it would be good to touch on that point a little more.
Code: Neglect of content of description

Text: I think it is good that the student is given specific instructions in a casual way so as not to discourage them.
